title: Mallotus sphaerocarpus
intro: Mallotus sphaerocarpus, also known as the Siva plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Mallotus sphaerocarpus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Mallotus sphaerocarpus is commonly processed into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions to treat various ailments, making it a valuable plant resource in Southeast Asian folk medicine.
taxonomy: Mallotus sphaerocarpus is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, commonly known as the spurge family. The Euphorbiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, but Mallotus sphaerocarpus is specifically classified within the tribe Acalyphinae. The plant is also classified as belonging to the order Malpighiales, which is a large order of flowering plants. The exact classification of Mallotus sphaerocarpus can be further broken down into more specific ranks such as genus and species.
